hero.headline: Building systems that don't break.
hero.description: Systems engineering student focused on backend architecture, CLI tooling and distributed systems. I build things from scratch and make them work.

about.bio: Systems Engineering student with hands-on experience in backend development and CLI tooling. I'm driven by the challenge of building software that works reliably — from architecture to packaging and deployment. Code is not art, but art can be made with code.
about.name: David Ricardo Nunez Bernal

projects.title: OpenLauncher
projects.tagline: Fully-featured Minecraft launcher with modloader support
projects.problem: Existing launchers lack fine-grained version control and modloader management in a single, portable tool.
projects.solution: Built a self-contained Python launcher with installer logic for Vanilla, Forge and Fabric, profile management and version switching.
keyDecisions: Stateless profile system using JSON manifests
Modular installer pipeline per loader type

projects.title: OpenCloud — File Blockchain
projects.tagline: Decentralized file storage with cryptographic integrity
projects.problem: Centralized cloud storage depends on trust. Files can be modified or deleted without the user knowing.
projects.solution: Implemented a blockchain-based file storage system in C# that splits responsibility between users and miners using AES encryption and hash chaining.
keyDecisions: Separation of user/miner roles for decentralization

projects.title: Persepolis
projects.tagline: Multi-source wallpaper search engine with AI categorization
projects.problem: Finding high-quality wallpapers requires visiting multiple sites manually with no unified interface.
projects.solution: Built a Java/Spring Boot web app that scrapes 9+ sources concurrently with AI-assisted tagging and a clean browsing UI.
keyDecisions: AI model integration for tag classification
Linux server deployment with reverse proxy

projects.title: DBT — Doesn't Break Things
projects.tagline: Local AI coding assistant powered by Ollama
projects.problem: Cloud-based AI coding tools require internet and send code to third-party servers — a problem for offline or sensitive projects.
projects.solution: Implemented a Claude Code-inspired system using local Ollama models, giving full AI coding assistance without external dependencies.
keyDecisions: Model-agnostic interface for any Ollama model
Local context management to fit model window

projects.title: Pixel-Kinesis
projects.tagline: A declarative DSL for graphics and animations
projects.problem: Creating programmatic animations requires low-level graphics code or heavy frameworks with no clean abstraction layer.
projects.solution: Designing a declarative Java-based DSL that compiles animation descriptions into rendered output, inspired by LaTeX.
keyDecisions: AST-based DSL parser for animation composition
Renderer abstraction layer for multiple backends

experiments.title: Distributed task queue
experiments.description: Exploring minimal distributed queue design without external brokers. Pure TCP + JSON protocol.

experiments.title: Zero-dependency static site generator
experiments.description: A minimal tool that compiles Markdown + templates to HTML with focus on build speed and portability.
